questions.question: Which HTML element should wrap a navigation bar for semantic and accessibility purposes?
questions.explanation: The nav element is a semantic HTML5 landmark that helps screen readers and search engines identify the primary navigation region.

questions.question: What CSS property removes the default bullets from a list used for navigation?
questions.explanation: list-style: none removes the default bullets from ul elements that are commonly used for navigation structures.

questions.question: To make navigation links full-width in a vertical nav, use:
questions.explanation: display: block on anchor links makes them fill the full width of their container, creating a larger clickable area.

questions.question: Which CSS layout method is recommended for modern horizontal navigation bars?
questions.explanation: Flexbox provides better alignment, spacing control, and responsive behavior for horizontal navigation than older float-based methods.

questions.question: How do you indicate the current page in a navigation bar?
options: Use a class like .active on the link
Use a different tag for the current link
questions.explanation: An .active class is applied to the current page link, typically via server-side logic or a template system, combined with aria-current='page'.

questions.question: What is the recommended ARIA attribute for the current page link in navigation?
questions.explanation: aria-current='page' tells screen readers that this link represents the current page, providing important navigation context.

questions.question: What CSS property should be used to create a sticky navigation bar that stays at the top during scroll?
questions.explanation: position: sticky with top: 0 keeps the nav in flow until the scroll threshold is reached, then it sticks. This is more flexible than fixed.

questions.question: What issue occurs when using a fixed navigation bar without adjusting the main content?
options: Content is overlapped by the nav bar
questions.explanation: Fixed nav bars are removed from normal flow and overlap the top of the page content. Adding padding-top to main content equal to the nav height fixes this.

questions.question: What is the minimum recommended touch target size for mobile navigation links?
questions.explanation: WCAG recommends touch targets of at least 44x44px to ensure comfortable tapping on mobile devices.

questions.question: Which selector is used to add dividers between navigation items with pseudo-elements?
questions.explanation: nav li:not(:last-child)::after adds a divider after every item except the last one, creating separators between navigation links.

questions.question: What is the purpose of a skip-to-content link in navigation?
options: To let keyboard users bypass the nav and go to main content
To skip to the next page
questions.explanation: A skip-to-content link allows keyboard and screen reader users to bypass repetitive navigation links and jump directly to the main content.

questions.question: Which of the following is a common mistake when styling navigation?
options: Removing outline on :focus without providing an alternative
questions.explanation: Removing outline: none without providing a visible focus indicator makes navigation impossible for keyboard users.

questions.question: What is the correct hover state CSS for a dark-themed navigation bar?
questions.explanation: A lighter background on hover provides visual feedback. For dark nav bars (#333), a medium gray (#575757) hover is common.

questions.question: What z-index value is recommended for a sticky navigation bar?
options: z-index is not needed for sticky
questions.explanation: A z-index of 100 is usually sufficient to keep the navigation above page content. Values that are too high can cause maintenance issues.

questions.question: How should a hamburger menu icon be implemented for mobile navigation?
options: All of the above are valid approaches
questions.explanation: Hamburger menus can use unicode characters, CSS-only checkbox hacks, or SVG icons, though JavaScript is needed for full keyboard accessibility.

questions.question: What property makes navigation items in a flex layout distribute space evenly?
questions.explanation: justify-content: space-between distributes items evenly along the main axis with space between each item, useful for nav bars with logo and links.

questions.question: What does the aria-label attribute on a nav element do?
options: Provides a descriptive label for screen readers
questions.explanation: aria-label provides an accessible name for the navigation landmark, useful when multiple nav elements exist on the same page.

questions.question: Why should float be avoided for navigation bar layouts?
options: Float doesn't work on list items
Flexbox is simpler, more reliable, and better for responsive design
questions.explanation: Flexbox provides better alignment, spacing, wrapping, and responsive capabilities than float-based navigation layouts.

questions.question: What CSS selector styles a navigation link that is being clicked?
questions.explanation: :active styles the element during the click, between mouse press and release, providing brief visual feedback.

questions.question: What is the correct HTML structure for a navigation bar?
questions.explanation: Semantic HTML structure uses <nav> with an unordered list (<ul>) containing list items (<li>) with anchor links (<a>), ensuring proper accessibility.
